threads.formatted_text:
--- Thread (46 messages, 2024-04-15) ---
[2024-04-15 02:00] aa01blue: wait how do you beat p2 in tatsu set
[2024-04-15 02:00] silentslayers: tatsu isn't even base set viable, can you give more context please?
[2024-04-15 02:01] silentslayers: I mean I remember msven covering that it worked in some sort of context, but I don't remember it being super relevant
[2024-04-15 02:01] aa01blue: Any ematrix tastu set
[2024-04-15 02:01] silentslayers: Like maybe it was asymetric access or something
[2024-04-15 02:01] aa01blue: Against DD DD for p2, you need to go 3. CC or something
[2024-04-15 02:01] silentslayers: Probably natural animus into tarsiers or tatsu depending on what they do?
[2024-04-15 02:02] aa01blue: doesn't natural animus die to 3. DAA
[2024-04-15 02:02] aa01blue: CC beats DAA handily
[2024-04-15 02:03] silentslayers: But this is a precise scenario
[2024-04-15 02:03] silentslayers: If P2 wins, so be it
[2024-04-15 02:03] silentslayers: P2 wont win every tatsu set for sure
[2024-04-15 02:03] silentslayers: matrix is very specific
[2024-04-15 02:03] silentslayers: But its probably natural animus as highest winning odds
[2024-04-15 02:03] silentslayers: then you tatsu or tars depending on response etc
[2024-04-15 02:04] silentslayers: like in base set this DAA dies
[2024-04-15 02:04] silentslayers: you just tars into rhinos
[2024-04-15 02:04] silentslayers: So that's the vacuum sitch
[2024-04-15 02:04] aa01blue: nvm natural animus wins apparently
[2024-04-15 02:06] silentslayers: wouldn't surprise me
[2024-04-15 02:08] aa01blue: I watched a video analyzing it but it was outdated
[2024-04-15 02:09] aa01blue: it's an ancient version of tastu with 3 health so you can get a second tatsu
[2024-04-15 02:09] aa01blue: since you only get breached for 2 after the rhino turn
[2024-04-15 02:12] aa01blue: and then tastu wins because tarsiers are more breach vulnerable than tatsus
[2024-04-15 02:13] aa01blue: Yeah modern tatsu is much more balanced then
[2024-04-15 02:13] aa01blue: (I can't believe they allowed that lol)
[2024-04-15 02:21] aa01blue: Slow conduit actually beats Tastu, even the 3hp version
[2024-04-15 02:22] silentslayers: But not with matrix, surely?
[2024-04-15 02:23] silentslayers: (Asymmetric access I guess)
[2024-04-15 02:31] aa01blue: It should still beat naked tatsu with 2hp? 🤔
[2024-04-15 02:31] silentslayers: my point is, if the tatsu player properly gets matrix in response to the green aggression
[2024-04-15 02:31] aa01blue: If it were 3hp tatsu you can go "sike" and go double blastforge the turn after nulli, and then spam ematrix
[2024-04-15 02:32] aa01blue: and somehow this wins but the green build order can maybe be improved
[2024-04-15 02:38] aa01blue: Oh looks like 2hp tatsu can still do that
[2024-04-15 02:39] aa01blue: So from what I calced, against DDC you can get a matrix and defend
[2024-04-15 02:41] silentslayers: With asymmetric access, it's usually super clear to see
[2024-04-15 02:41] silentslayers: If both have tatsu, analyzing becomes usually more difficult
[2024-04-15 02:41] silentslayers: that's why it's easier to prove a unit's viability instead of which player position wins in a baseset + 1
[2024-04-15 02:42] silentslayers: I've analyzed a lot of unit's viability with asymmetric access, but not nearly as many symmetric access baseset +1s
[2024-04-15 02:43] silentslayers: And both are quite educational. 🙂
[2024-04-15 02:47] aa01blue: Analyzing two units' viability is also really hard
[2024-04-15 02:48] aa01blue: it seems like natural animus beats tatsu ematrix but i haven't checked every possible timing where you get breached again for another tatsu
[2024-04-15 02:48] aa01blue: Probably safe to assume it's impossible cuz that  you fall down to 10 drones
[2024-04-15 02:49] aa01blue: actually, 2 more drones die to protect matrix so 8
[2024-04-15 02:50] aa01blue: Calculating opening lines is fun, it reminds me of cruncing openers in Advance wars by web
[2024-04-15 02:54] silentslayers: 2 unit viability isn't hard if one player gets access to both, and the other zero. But yeah if you do other permutations, e.g. 1 player gets matrix and the other gets Tatsu + Matrix, then it's not necessarily easy, yes.

threads.formatted_text:
--- Thread (7 messages, 2024-04-16) ---
[2024-04-16 07:38] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): buildtime and exhaust aren't considered strictly negative statuses for SDR because they sometimes have benefits (e.g. gold armor or synced iso kronuses) unlike stamina/lifespan/damaged fragile units/etc
correct that exhausted/building units don't lose lifespan which is why i avoid the combination for custom units since it's not immediately apparent to players
[2024-04-16 07:42] aa01blue: true i forgot exhaust isn't always negative
[2024-04-16 07:43] aa01blue: Also the exhaust keyword being on units that normally don't have it opens up some interesting ideas
[2024-04-16 07:45] aa01blue: Maybe some effects that exhaust units, like:
Click: Exhaust a Wall to gain 1 :Attack:
[2024-04-16 07:46] aa01blue: wait no that's just a steelsplitter
[2024-04-16 09:58] sano712 (sano): why isn't exhaust strictly negative? sure, syncing isos is nice, but they would still be strictly better if they attacked every turn
[2024-04-16 09:58] sano712 (sano): basically is there any scenario where it's preferable to have less attack?

messages.author: randomflyingtaco
messages.content:
buildtime and exhaust aren't considered strictly negative statuses for SDR because they sometimes have benefits (e.g. gold armor or synced iso kronuses) unlike stamina/lifespan/damaged fragile units/etc
correct that exhausted/building units don't lose lifespan which is why i avoid the combination for custom units since it's not immediately apparent to players

messages.author: aa01blue
messages.content: true i forgot exhaust isn't always negative

messages.author: aa01blue
messages.content: Also the exhaust keyword being on units that normally don't have it opens up some interesting ideas

messages.author: aa01blue
messages.content:
Maybe some effects that exhaust units, like:
Click: Exhaust a Wall to gain 1 :Attack:

messages.author: aa01blue
messages.content: wait no that's just a steelsplitter

messages.author: sano712
messages.content: why isn't exhaust strictly negative? sure, syncing isos is nice, but they would still be strictly better if they attacked every turn

messages.author: sano712
messages.content: basically is there any scenario where it's preferable to have less attack?

threads.formatted_text:
--- Thread (5 messages, 2024-04-16) ---
[2024-04-16 11:27] wibbie: I don't think there is if you assume players are fairly rational
[2024-04-16 11:28] wibbie: You might argue that players that are of lower skill / absent-minded may forget about the isos your opponent bought last turn
[2024-04-16 11:29] wibbie: as it doesn't show up in the attack counter
[2024-04-16 11:29] wibbie: and not buy pre-emptive soak they should buy
[2024-04-16 11:29] wibbie: Though I guess this is only 'worth' it over more attack when you are below the absorb barrier

messages.author: wibbie
messages.content: I don't think there is if you assume players are fairly rational

messages.author: wibbie
messages.content: You might argue that players that are of lower skill / absent-minded may forget about the isos your opponent bought last turn

messages.author: wibbie
messages.content: as it doesn't show up in the attack counter

messages.author: wibbie
messages.content: and not buy pre-emptive soak they should buy

messages.author: wibbie
messages.content: Though I guess this is only 'worth' it over more attack when you are below the absorb barrier

--- Thread (14 messages, 2024-04-16) ---
[2024-04-16 12:49] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): sometimes it's preferable to have delayed attack
[2024-04-16 12:49] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): well, in almost every set, before absorb threshold is reached
[2024-04-16 12:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): though I guess if two units cost the same, then before absorb threshold it makes no difference which version you buy, and afterwards the immediate attacker is better
[2024-04-16 12:51] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but if their cost reflects the difference, then before absorb threshold (or in similar board states where cheaper delayed attack is more efficient), you would buy the delayed version
[2024-04-16 12:51] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): nvm I didn't read your last message
[2024-04-16 12:55] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I was trying to think of a situation where you would rather NOT damage your opponent
[2024-04-16 12:55] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): and I think I found one
[2024-04-16 12:55] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): regenerating units and upcoming additional attack / apollo
[2024-04-16 12:55] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): where if you damage them now they will regenerate
[2024-04-16 12:55] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I guess that is essentially similar to absorb though
[2024-04-16 12:57] lyra1712 (Lyra): im confused, surely it is obvious you would rather have some amount of gauss charges instead of 0 of them, always ?
[2024-04-16 12:57] lyra1712 (Lyra): if thats not what you mean i don't seem to know what you're talking about
[2024-04-16 13:01] w1tch0r: With perfect play, I also cant think of a scenario where less attack is better. You could maybe bait an opponent into a bad play or they need to manual defend because Q doesnt work optimal.
[2024-04-16 13:03] lyra1712 (Lyra): i doubt even q defense would fuck up

--- Thread (6 messages, 2024-04-16) ---
[2024-04-16 13:48] sano712 (sano): The point is build time is not strictly bad because there's the edge case where golden armor is valuable
[2024-04-16 13:49] sano712 (sano): Exhaust on the other is strictly worse than no exhaust, I can't think of any situation where not having attack is better than having attack
[2024-04-16 13:52] lyra1712 (Lyra): if you mean iso chronus that would deal 2 dmg every turn is strictly better than regular iso, i agree. but you definitely sometimes prefer your regular iso to be on exhaust 2 instead of 1
[2024-04-16 13:56] sano712 (sano): Is there a scenario where you prefer your iso to be on exhaust 2 instead of 1 and it's not because of other exhaust units?
[2024-04-16 13:57] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Syncing with antima?
[2024-04-16 13:57] sano712 (sano): hmm I guess if exhaust can synergize with build time and build time is not strictly bad then exhaust is not strictly bad? 😓

messages.author: sano712
messages.content: The point is build time is not strictly bad because there's the edge case where golden armor is valuable

messages.author: sano712
messages.content: Exhaust on the other is strictly worse than no exhaust, I can't think of any situation where not having attack is better than having attack

messages.author: lyra1712
messages.content: if you mean iso chronus that would deal 2 dmg every turn is strictly better than regular iso, i agree. but you definitely sometimes prefer your regular iso to be on exhaust 2 instead of 1

messages.author: sano712
messages.content: Is there a scenario where you prefer your iso to be on exhaust 2 instead of 1 and it's not because of other exhaust units?

messages.author: randomflyingtaco
messages.content: Syncing with antima?

messages.author: sano712
messages.content: hmm I guess if exhaust can synergize with build time and build time is not strictly bad then exhaust is not strictly bad? 😓

threads.formatted_text:
--- Thread (21 messages, 2024-04-16) ---
[2024-04-16 17:39] silentslayers: yeah i think its kinda unfair to say exhaust is not strictly bad. Because otherwise, without exhaust, it'd attack every turn
[2024-04-16 17:40] silentslayers: If we're talking about designing and balancing a unit, sure that's another thing
[2024-04-16 17:40] silentslayers: But slapping an exhaust onto tarsier will always make it worse
[2024-04-16 17:40] lyra1712 (Lyra): theres 2 reasonable points to make, and saying "exhaust is/is not strictly bad" is ambiguous between which one you're talking about
[2024-04-16 17:41] silentslayers: I assumed the topic was SDR
[2024-04-16 17:41] lyra1712 (Lyra): i agree with the point you just made ofc
[2024-04-16 17:41] lyra1712 (Lyra): and the second point, which you are not making, but which is also valid, is :
if an existing exhaust unit is on the board, having it on 1 exhaust in not strictly better than 2 exhaust
[2024-04-16 17:42] lyra1712 (Lyra): cf iso/chrono/whatever
[2024-04-16 17:43] lyra1712 (Lyra): and this is also related to sdr
[2024-04-16 17:43] lyra1712 (Lyra): you always want more lifespan/fragile hp, you don't always want less (current turn) exhaust
[2024-04-16 17:49] sano712 (sano): yeah from the discussion above it's easy to show that `exhaust n` is not strictly better than `exhaust m` for `n < m`, but exhaust is still strictly worse than no exhaust.
[2024-04-16 17:49] silentslayers: It's a funny concept. Because once you factor in balance and design, you could argue you want less fragile HP. Since there'd be a trade off in efficiency if balanced well. Like nivo could negate a higher HP thing that you'd pay more for.
[2024-04-16 17:49] sano712 (sano): (I guess I should have added `n > 0`)
[2024-04-16 17:49] silentslayers: edit it in, I saw nothing :LongNoseLiar:
[2024-04-16 17:51] silentslayers: And for example you'd always want an exhaust 4 unit over an exhaust 8, where they are twice the amount of exhaust, right? But not necessarily exhaust 3 versus exhaust 5
[2024-04-16 17:52] silentslayers: It's also a funny thought for me, because it's like "Take this strictly bad tag we can put on two things, now see that sometimes you want more of this strictly bad tag sometimes!!!" XD
[2024-04-16 17:52] silentslayers: Like sure you've proven it isn't strictly bad if you have two things having a ShiTty tag of differing amounts
[2024-04-16 17:53] silentslayers: I'm just giving weird thoughts btw, I agree with everything you wrote Lyra.
[2024-04-16 17:56] lyra1712 (Lyra): sure, but thats a different point altogether
[2024-04-16 17:57] lyra1712 (Lyra): also, lower exhaust count is always better on clickable things
[2024-04-16 20:06] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): you can construct scenarios where exhaust X+lifespan Y is better than lifespan Y, so exhaust isn't strictly worse than no exhaust

messages.content: yeah from the discussion above it's easy to show that `exhaust n` is not strictly better than `exhaust m` for `n < m`, but exhaust is still strictly worse than no exhaust.

messages.author: silentslayers

[2024-04-16 21:36] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Sure, both players have breached and now P1 has 2 Animuses (Animii?) and 2 LS3 Grimbotch, P2 only has a LS3 Doomed Mech. P2 would win if the Doomed Mech also had the status Exhaust 2 meaning it can't be a strictly negative status

1, 2, 3, 4, ... gold?
[2024-04-27 09:43] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): so it's infinitely better than 2 drones
[2024-04-27 09:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): and you don't even pay energy for it
[2024-04-27 09:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): and you don't even need to buy tech structures for the big legendaries
[2024-04-27 09:45] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): or maybe I misunderstood what the non-deplete means
[2024-04-27 09:46] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): maybe it still gets used up but you can store it? nvm
[2024-04-27 09:46] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): in that case it's not as giga OP but potentially quite good with some awkward tech cost units
[2024-04-27 09:48] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): tbh for me any unit that synergizes with 3+ red units is too OP lol
[2024-04-27 09:48] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): those units are already giga dominant the moment they have any synergy
[2024-04-27 09:49] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): and not just the 3+ red units, but wincer and antima can get stupid too with synergies
[2024-04-27 09:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): where on one turn it's bad to go for it, and the next it's extremely good, and one side is just shrekt by the nature of discrete turns
[2024-04-27 09:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): or sometimes, one turn it's not possible to go for it, and the next it is, and the earliest timing is best
[2024-04-27 09:52] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): maybe base set needs more bp support, idk
[2024-04-27 09:52] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): maybe that helps alleviate these problems
[2024-04-27 09:53] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): though does bp do much vs antima anyway?
[2024-04-27 11:39] Deleted User (Deleted User): Can only substitute for one resource at a time, so not all of them at once, think of it as a green that you can use to pay for red or blue or use as gold
[2024-04-27 11:42] silentslayers: doesnt seem strong to me at all. just a rare combo unit for RRR/RRRR/BB/BBB type stuff. If it really is OP, make it cost just one more. No way it's base set viable for starters. Wouldn't be surprised if it needs to cost one less.
[2024-04-27 11:47] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): why does it matter if it's base set viable if it has insane synergies with certain units
[2024-04-27 11:47] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): a unit that is bad in general, but makes the game completely one-sided in rare cases sounds like bad design
[2024-04-27 11:48] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): so surely base set viability and such is not the main concern
[2024-04-27 11:48] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): more so than it just being extremely one-sided with certain synergies
[2024-04-27 11:48] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): unless I misunderstood your point

threads.formatted_text:
--- Thread (5 messages, 2024-08-09) ---
[2024-08-09 22:42] lazyanttu: Do you have some database/excel that would contain all data from the unit, preferably something that could be interfaced nicely with (API ideally, but can be a file) and that would contain all unit data for programmatic unit analysis 🙂

Best I could find was: https://prismata.fandom.com/wiki/Unit

But it seems to lack some units, so I don't know how much it can be trusted (for example, it does not contain the Forcefield which is base set unit), and it lacks some vital fields, such as that they consume Drones.
Unit
Unit is a generic term that covers both the available true units and spells in Prismata.
[2024-08-09 22:43] lazyanttu: Or anything that would contain the full data of multiplayer units in some table/json etc. format. Preferably:
-Complete list
-All fields, necessary information
[2024-08-09 22:46] lyra1712 (Lyra): i don't think there is, unfortunately
[2024-08-09 22:47] lyra1712 (Lyra): at least, not available to the community, obv the devs could have something like that
[2024-08-09 22:49] lyra1712 (Lyra): but if you do something by hand anyway, id be curious to see the results

messages.author: lazyanttu
messages.content:

Best I could find was: https://prismata.fandom.com/wiki/Unit

But it seems to lack some units, so I don't know how much it can be trusted (for example, it does not contain the Forcefield which is base set unit), and it lacks some vital fields, such as that they consume Drones.
Unit
Unit is a generic term that covers both the available true units and spells in Prismata.

messages.content:
Or anything that would contain the full data of multiplayer units in some table/json etc. format. Preferably:

-All fields, necessary information

--- Thread (28 messages, 2024-08-10) ---
[2024-08-10 00:15] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I dug a little in the swf and there is _something_
[2024-08-10 00:16] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but I don't think it's _the_ thing
[2024-08-10 00:16] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): it seems (if I understood correctly) that the unit info is received from the server
[2024-08-10 00:16] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but there is some test data baked into the swf: https://discord.com/channels/112616041175089152/406260513312604160/1271608120342351883
[2024-08-10 00:16] surfinite: Dave Churchill created Prismata's the AI.  Lunarch and Dave had some kind of agreement where Dave could use Prismata as a case study in his PhD, and also in the lectures he subsequently gave about AI for video games.
There are full versions of some of his lectures on youtube :
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=d0BOs8SO2bA

At the end of this video, Dave runs what looks to be a custom client:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uJtduCR9PNI
Which he uploaded the source code for to github:
https://github.com/davechurchill/PrismataAI
I don't actually know for sure, but I believe it would have to have unit details somewhere in the code.
[2024-08-10 00:17] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): oops we posted at the same time, meant to paste this along my messages above ^-^
[2024-08-10 00:21] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Nice, I think this is what OP is looking for
https://github.com/davechurchill/PrismataAI/blob/master/bin/asset/config/cardLibrary.jso
[2024-08-10 00:23] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but it seems to be missing things
[2024-08-10 00:24] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): e.g. nothing found for "bombarder"
[2024-08-10 00:24] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Whereas the test thing I extracted from the SWF that I posted above has Bombarder, for example
[2024-08-10 00:24] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but idk how up-to-date even that test data from the SWF is, didn't read through it
[2024-08-10 00:25] surfinite: For a long while now I've been thinking about whether it would be possible to train an AI with more modern tools/techniques that would be better than current masterbot.  I was always thinking that Dave's code on Github is what I'd use as a starting point if I could ever had the motivation to try it out.
[2024-08-10 00:30] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): It seems from row 1424 there are some interesting things:
[2024-08-10 00:30] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): hundreds of units I've never heard of before
[2024-08-10 00:31] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): idk if hundreds, cos a lot of them are prolly the event units
[2024-08-10 00:32] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but still, quite a few things I haven't heard of before in there
[2024-08-10 00:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): ```"Frost Tarsier" : {
    "rarity" : "normal",
    "toughness" : 2,
    "defaultBlocking" : 0,
    "assignedBlocking" : 0,
    "buyCost" : "3C",
    "beginOwnTurnScript" : {"receive" : "A"},
    "targetAction" : "disrupt",
    "targetAmount" : 2,
    "buySac" : [["Tarsier"]],
    "needs" : ["Tarsier"]
},```
[2024-08-10 00:34] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): `"targetAction" : "disrupt",` seems to be Chill
[2024-08-10 00:34] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): e.g.
```"Cryo Ray" : {
    "rarity" : "normal",
    "toughness" : 3,
    "fragile" : 1,
    "defaultBlocking" : 0,
    "assignedBlocking" : 0,
    "buyCost" : "1G",
    "targetAction" : "disrupt",
    "HPUsed" : 1,
    "targetAmount" : 1,
    "UIName" : "Cryo Ray"
},```
[2024-08-10 00:34] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): so Frost Tarsier is a 3C spell that upgrades a Tarsier into a 2 HP Tarsier that can freeze for 2
[2024-08-10 00:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): ```"Nightmare Vortex" : {
    "spell" : 1,
    "rarity" : "rare",
    "toughness" : 1,
    "defaultBlocking" : 0,
    "buyCost" : "1HGBC",
    "buildTime" : 0,
    "buyScript" : {"create" : [["Cryo Cell", "own", 15, 4]]},
    "needs" : ["Cryo Cell"],
    "UIShortname" : "NM. Vortex"
},```
[2024-08-10 00:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): ```"Cryo Cell" : {
    "rarity" : "trinket",
    "toughness" : 1,
    "defaultBlocking" : 0,
    "assignedBlocking" : 0,
    "buyCost" : "G",
    "abilityScript" : {"selfsac" : true},
    "targetAction" : "disrupt",
    "targetAmount" : 1,
    "lifespan" : 1,
    "UIName" : "Cryo Cell"
},```
[2024-08-10 00:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): here is Redeemer:
```"Redeemer" : {
    "rarity" : "rare",
    "toughness" : 4,
    "defaultBlocking" : 1,
    "assignedBlocking" : 0,
    "buyCost" : "10GB",
    "abilityScript" : {"receive" : "AAA"},
    "buyScript" : {"create" : [["Gauss Charge", "opponent", 6,3]]},
    "needs" : ["Gauss Charge"],
    "UIName" : "Redeemer",
    "score" : "15CCC"
},```
[2024-08-10 00:39] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): In Redeemer we have:
` "buyScript" : {"create" : [["Gauss Charge", "opponent", 6,3]]},` 
Which give oppo 6 charges 3 turns from now

So I guess Nightmare Vortex's one gives us 15 Cryo Cells 4 turns from now
`    "buyScript" : {"create" : [["Cryo Cell", "own", 15, 4]]},`
[2024-08-10 00:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): and Cryo Cells seem to be lifespan1 freeze1 units
[2024-08-10 00:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): anyway, cool stuff in there
[2024-08-10 00:42] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Yea I've fantasized about such a project too

It would be really cool to have a super strong Prismata AI that consistently beats top players

threads.formatted_text:
--- Thread (4 messages, 2024-10-19) ---
[2024-10-19 15:35] megasupp (mega-supp): I just read the rules for Arimaa and it seems way harder than prismata from a computational standpoint
[2024-10-19 15:37] megasupp (mega-supp): Thing is prismata is fundamentally a game about resource management, the resources being gold, green, blue, red, attack, absorb, soak, energy
[2024-10-19 15:43] megasupp (mega-supp): You can model most units as trade offs between different resources and then you can boil the game down to choosing between some few distinct strategies
[2024-10-19 15:46] megasupp (mega-supp): In Ariima there seem to be no simple heuristic to determine who's winning

messages.author: megasupp
messages.content: I just read the rules for Arimaa and it seems way harder than prismata from a computational standpoint

messages.content: Thing is prismata is fundamentally a game about resource management, the resources being gold, green, blue, red, attack, absorb, soak, energy

messages.author: megasupp
messages.content: You can model most units as trade offs between different resources and then you can boil the game down to choosing between some few distinct strategies

messages.author: megasupp
messages.content: In Ariima there seem to be no simple heuristic to determine who's winning

threads.formatted_text:
--- Thread (5 messages, 2024-10-19 to 2024-10-20) ---
[2024-10-19 16:04] liquideggproduct (Liquid Egg Product): Haven’t had a chance to play Arimaa, but there was a software that managed to beat top humans even a decade ago. 

For a game that was designed to be anti-computer, that had to be disappointingly quick. 

https://www.kingpinchess.net/2015/07/arimaa-game-over/
Arimaa: Game Over?
[2024-10-19 16:09] liquideggproduct (Liquid Egg Product): I’d love to find more about the history. Unlike chess, where multiple programs are better than humans, Sharp is apparently head-and-shoulders above everything else.
[2024-10-19 16:10] liquideggproduct (Liquid Egg Product): I wonder what their developers figured out.
[2024-10-20 06:20] aa01blue: the arimaa challenge ran for a good number of years
[2024-10-20 06:21] aa01blue: what's funny is that the top players now can beat the winning bot, and there hasn't been any stronger versions iirc

messages.author: liquideggproduct
messages.content:
Haven’t had a chance to play Arimaa, but there was a software that managed to beat top humans even a decade ago. 

For a game that was designed to be anti-computer, that had to be disappointingly quick. 

https://www.kingpinchess.net/2015/07/arimaa-game-over/
Arimaa: Game Over?

messages.author: liquideggproduct
messages.content: I’d love to find more about the history. Unlike chess, where multiple programs are better than humans, Sharp is apparently head-and-shoulders above everything else.

messages.author: liquideggproduct
messages.content: I wonder what their developers figured out.

messages.author: aa01blue
messages.content: the arimaa challenge ran for a good number of years
